votes.reason: The Python code is a direct translation of the Coq implementation. It correctly implements the recursive logic for handling single versus consecutive spaces and the helper function `skip_spaces` behaves identically to the Coq version.
The Python code is a direct and faithful translation of the Coq implementation, preserving the logic for replacing spaces and skipping consecutive spaces.
The Python code directly translates the Coq recursive functions and logic, including the fuel-based recursion and space-skipping logic, ensuring identical behavior on all inputs.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq implementation. Both use the same logic: skip_spaces removes leading spaces, fix_spaces_func processes the list with fuel, replacing single spaces with underscore and 2+ consecutive spaces with a single dash while skipping remaining spaces. The fuel is set to len(l)+1 in both. The helper functions and main logic match exactly, so concrete inputs will produce the same results.

The Python code faithfully translates the Coq implementation. The skip_spaces function removes leading spaces identically. The fix_spaces_func uses the same logic: single space becomes underscore, two or more consecutive spaces become a single dash (skipping remaining spaces), and non-space characters pass through unchanged. The fuel parameter and recursion structure match. Both return the same concrete results for any input string.

The fix_spaces_func uses the same logic: single space becomes underscore, two or more consecutive spaces become a single dash (skipping remaining spaces). The fuel parameter and recursion structure match. Both convert string to list of characters, process, and convert back. The pre-condition is trivially True, and the spec checks equality with fix_spaces output in both versions.

The Python code mirrors the Coq logic: single spaces become '_', runs of two or more spaces collapse to '-', with the same skip of consecutive spaces and sufficient fuel (len(s)+1). String/list conversions align, so outputs match for all inputs.
